description:
Goal: one revision change == one publication == one defined/update event. Signal: a new Compiler2 quicksort trace-summary handler reports zero duplicate state-change emissions, and the raw quicksort trace emits `semantic_closed.defined` and `materialized_program.defined` once per distinct revision. Strategy: add a Compiler2-specific test-only telemetry summary that downcasts raw ids/facts/jobs in the handler, then refactor Compiler2-owned slot maps and world define helpers so change information originates at the ownership boundary and no-op replays stay silent.

Change:
- Add a test-only Compiler2 trace-summary handler; unlike `StatsHandler`, it counts spans, sums elapsed time, groups exact jobs, detects duplicate subject+revision emissions, counts pre-drive frontend work, and measures analyzed activations that do not survive into the final closure.
- Introduce a Compiler2-owned state-change result at the map/world boundary instead of returning only a revision.
- Emit `*.defined` / `*.updated` telemetry only when the underlying state actually changes.
- Tighten tests around revision/event honesty so same-state replays stay silent and the trace summary reports zero duplicate publications.

Why:
- Same-revision republishes make the trace lie and hide the real fixpoint churn.
- Raw event counts alone are too blunt; this arc needs a direct measure of waste.

Checks:
- Quicksort trace-summary reports zero duplicate `semantic_closed`, `materialized_program`, and activation/callsite/return emissions.
- The ignored JSONL dump still matches the summary view when we need a deep inspection.
title: Compiler2 signal honesty: publish and emit only on real state change
